description_long: This is a shallow, round bowl with a wide opening and a slightly flared rim, supported by a thick, circular pedestal base. The exterior surface appears to be a weathered, earthy brown with visible textural irregularities, suggesting it is made from fired clay or ceramic. The interior is decorated with a dark, almost black background and features a repeating geometric meander pattern along the upper rim, as well as stylized motifs, possibly anthropomorphic or zoomorphic, in the center. The bowl shows signs of age and use, with some surface discoloration and minor abrasions. The overall form is symmetrical, and the base is broad enough to provide stability. This object likely has both functional and decorative value, hinting at traditional or historical craftsmanship.
description: A shallow, round ceramic bowl with a wide opening, decorated with geometric and figurative patterns on a dark interior and supported by a thick pedestal base.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from a side angle, highlighting the flared rim, pedestal base, and the geometric pattern along the inner rim.
description_view_1: This view shows more of the interior, revealing the dark surface and the start of the central motif, with the geometric border clearly visible.
description_view_2: The interior motif is most visible here, showing a stylized figure or symbol in the center, surrounded by the geometric border.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the bowl is shown, with the rim and base visible, and the geometric pattern continuing along the inner edge.
attribute_location: ceremonial space or traditional dining area
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric meander pattern along the inner rim and a stylized central motif, possibly anthropomorphic or zoomorphic, on the interior base.
